q: Photosynthesis structures: Where do the light-dependent and light-independent (Calvin cycle) reactions occur within the chloroplast?
options: Both reactions occur in the stroma; the thylakoid membrane is used only for pigment storage
Light-dependent reactions occur in the thylakoid membranes (and thylakoid lumen); Calvin cycle reactions occur in the stroma
Light-dependent reactions occur in the stroma; Calvin cycle reactions occur on the outer chloroplast membrane
Both reactions occur in the thylakoid lumen; the stroma is used only for structural support
explanation: Light reactions (photolysis, ATP and NADPH production) occur at the thylakoid membrane; the Calvin cycle (CO2 fixation using ATP and NADPH) occurs in the fluid-filled stroma surrounding the thylakoids.
